targetRange.populationContext: individualized targets, <7.0% for high CV risk patients

labs.actionRequired: adjust therapy if targets not achieved
labs.clinicalContext: Monitor to assess achievement of individualized glycemic targets and glucose-lowering efficacy

labs.initialMonitoring: frequent when starting with hypoglycemia-causing agents
labs.actionRequired: reduce doses of sulfonylurea/insulin if hypoglycemia risk
labs.clinicalContext: Monitor for hypoglycemia risk when used with sulfonylureas or insulin
GLP-1 RA promote weight loss, particularly beneficial for patients with obesity
Monitor kidney function as GLP-1 RA may slow eGFR decline and have kidney protective benefits
GLP-1 RA reduce albuminuria and may be useful for reducing albuminuria in CKD patients

labRequirements.specialPopulationNotes: GLP-1 RA can be used in patients with eGFR as low as 15 ml/min/1.73 m². Limited data for severe CKD. Contraindicated in patients with history of medullary thyroid cancer, MEN-2, or acute pancreatitis. Most require subcutaneous injection except oral semaglutide.
labRequirements.sourceSnippet: In patients with T2D and CKD who have not achieved individualized glycemic targets despite use of metformin and SGLT2i treatment, or who are unable to use those medications, we recommend a long-acting GLP-1 RA (1B). The risk of hypoglycemia is increased when GLP-1 RA are used in combination with sulfonylureas or insulin. GLP-1 RA reduce albuminuria and may slow eGFR decline.

governance.sourceDocument: KDIGO 2022 Clinical Practice Guideline for Diabetes Management in Chronic Kidney Disease
